what_went_well: Clear reference material: archived telegram-bot-integration tasks.md section 7 and delivery-channels spec provided precise requirements
Existing TelegramSender implementation is clean and well-documented — adapter design maps directly to its interface
Spec delta covers all edge cases: success, failure, missing config, network errors, address parsing variants
Design decisions are grounded in existing codebase patterns (namespace placement, service tagging)
improvement_suggestions: The ChannelAdapterInterface and delivery VOs (DeliveryPayload, DeliveryTarget, DeliveryResult) do not exist in the codebase yet — the add-delivery-channels proposal is archived but not implemented. Implementation of this adapter is blocked until those are created.
Consider whether the adapter should also support TelegramSender::sendPhoto() for card-type payloads with media — currently scoped to text-only
blocked_by: add-delivery-channels implementation (ChannelAdapterInterface, DeliveryPayload, DeliveryTarget, DeliveryResult VOs must exist before this adapter can be coded)
deviations_from_spec: The archived telegram-outbound-messaging spec defines the requirement under that capability; this proposal adds the delta to delivery-channels capability instead, since the adapter is an extension of the delivery-channels adapter pattern rather than a standalone telegram-outbound concern
